decks.notes: The deck relies on using creatures with various abilities to search for specific creatures to deal damage and control the opponent's board. It combines creature toolbox elements with combo potential to create multiple win conditions. By utilizing cards like Green Sun's Zenith and Chord of Calling, the deck can tutor for key creatures like Yawgmoth, Thran Physician and Walking Ballista to pull off game-winning plays. The main strategy revolves around assembling the right combination of creatures to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory through different avenues.

This deck focuses on putting pressure on the opponent with aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury while disrupting their game plan with cards like Prismatic Ending and Teferi, Time Raveler. It also uses control elements like Solitude and Consign to Memory to maintain board presence. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while steadily building up a threatening board presence, utilizing a mix of aggressive creatures and versatile spells to gain an advantage and secure the win.

The deck focuses on using aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd to pressure the opponent while disrupting their game plan with cards like Prismatic Ending and Solitude. It also utilizes cards like Quantum Riddler and Consign to Memory to generate advantage and control the board. Additionally, the deck includes lands that provide flexibility and allow for good mana fixing. Overall, the deck aims to apply pressure, disrupt the opponent's strategy, and maintain control of the game to secure a victory.

This deck utilizes a mix of aggressive creatures and disruptive spells to control the board and apply pressure on the opponent. It includes cards that can deal with threats efficiently and protect your own creatures, allowing you to maintain tempo and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The combination of aggressive creatures like Ragavan and powerful removal spells like Solitude and Galvanic Discharge makes this deck a versatile and deadly force to be reckoned with in a Modern 1 vs 1 format.

This deck aims to quickly ramp up mana and use Amulet of Vigor to take advantage of the untap abilities of lands like Simic Growth Chamber and Gruul Turf. The goal is to utilize Primeval Titan to search for lands like Vesuva and bounce lands to create large amounts of mana in order to cast a game-ending Scapeshift or Green Sun's Zenith. The deck also includes cards like Aftermath Analyst and Urza's Saga to provide additional value and card advantage. The key strategy is to assemble the necessary pieces to generate tremendous amounts of mana and overwhelm the opponent with powerful combo plays.

The main concept of this Boros Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of powerful creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury, as well as efficient removal spells like Galvanic Discharge and Thraben Charm. The deck also utilizes Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Arena of Glory to generate additional value and maintain board control. Additionally, cards like Guide of Souls and Fable of the Mirror-Breaker provide card advantage and synergy within the deck. Overall, this deck aims to apply pressure early and maintain tempo throughout the game to secure a quick victory.

The main focus of this Boros Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and direct damage spells. The deck utilizes cards like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment to deal damage directly to the opponent, while also using creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to put pressure on the opponent early in the game. Ajani, Nactl Pariah and Voice of Victory provide additional support and buffs for the team. The deck aims to close out the game quickly with a mix of aggressive creatures and burn spells.

This deck focuses on putting pressure on the opponent with fast and efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd. It also disrupts the opponent's game plan with cards like Quantum Riddler and Consign to Memory. The deck also has control elements like Solitude and Prismatic Ending to handle threats. Additionally, the deck can generate card advantage and protect its creatures with cards like Ephemerate and Teferi, Time Raveler. Overall, the strategy is to continuously apply pressure while disrupting the opponent's strategy to secure a victory.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggression and quickly overwhelming the opponent with a mix of strong creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury. The deck includes powerful support cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Goblin Bombardment to maintain pressure and control the board. Additional protection and card advantage come from cards like Ranger-Captain of Eos and Seasoned Pyromancer. The deck also includes a small land disruption package with cards like Blood Moon to disrupt opponents' mana bases. Overall, the deck aims to quickly close out games with its aggressive creatures and strong synergies.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creature plays and direct damage to overwhelm opponents quickly. It utilizes cards like Ajani, Guide of Souls, and Ragavan to generate threats on the battlefield, while also using spells like Galvanic Discharge and Thraben Charm to deal damage directly to the opponent. The deck also includes cards like Ocelot Pride and Phlage to boost the power of its creatures. With a mix of creature power and burn spells, this deck aims to close out the game swiftly before the opponent can establish a strong defense.

The main focus of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's strategy through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage. The deck aims to maintain board control and out-value the opponent by utilizing cards like Consult the Star Charts for card selection, Orcish Bowmasters for removal, and Wan Shi Tong, Librarian for card draw. With a mix of efficient removal spells and powerful control tools like Counterspell, the deck seeks to slow down the opponent's game plan and ultimately gain the upper hand in the long game.

Utilize Amulet of Vigor to quickly ramp and search for Primeval Titan, allowing you to search for key lands like Vesuva and Tolaria West. Use lands like Simic Growth Chamber and Crumbling Vestige to generate more mana and trigger additional landfall abilities. Combo with cards like Scapeshift and Lotus Field to create massive turns where you can search for multiple lands and overwhelm your opponent with powerful land synergies. Use Aftermath Analyst for card advantage and disruption, while Boseiju, Who Endures protects your spells from countermagic. Win through powerful land synergies and overwhelming your opponent with massive threats.

The main concept of this UR Control deck is to utilize a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw to control the game and outvalue the opponent. The deck focuses on disruption by countering key spells, dealing with threats, and generating card advantage through spells like Expressive Iteration and Mishra's Bauble. It also aims to finish the game with powerful threats like Snapcaster Mage and Slash, Reptile Rampager. Overall, the deck aims to control the board and the opponent's actions to secure the win.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly establish a board presence with aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd. The deck also aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan through cards like Consign to Memory and Prismatic Ending. Additionally, the deck utilizes cards like Quantum Riddler and Teferi, Time Raveler to control the tempo of the game. The inclusion of cards like Solitude and Wrath of the Skies provide additional removal options to keep the opponent's board in check. The deck aims to apply pressure while disrupting the opponent's strategy, ultimately leading to victory.

This deck focuses on assembling UrzaTron lands (Urza's Mine, Power Plant, and Tower) to generate a large amount of mana quickly. It aims to control the board with powerful planeswalkers like Ugin and Karn, while also disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Thought-Knot Seer and Kozilek's Command. Additionally, it looks to win through card advantage and efficient removal spells, using Eldrazi creatures like Devourer of Destiny and Glaring Fleshraker to pressure the opponent. The main goal is to overwhelm and outvalue the opponent by leveraging the mana advantage provided by UrzaTron lands.

This deck focuses on playing aggressively while disrupting the opponent's game plan with taxing effects. It utilizes powerful creatures like Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd to pressure the opponent, while also using disruptive spells like Galvanic Discharge and Prismatic Ending to control the board. The inclusion of cards like Ephemerate and Consign to Memory allows for flexibility and resource management. By combining aggression with disruption, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and secure a quick victory in a Modern 1 vs 1 format.
